preamble: Vocalization of the voiced dental fricative [ð] to the close back rounded vowel [u] or its corresponding semivowel [w] occurs in some Romance dialects and English varieties as a form of extreme lenition.

examples.note: Final [ð] (from /d/) vocalizes to [u] or [w] in many Southern Spanish and Latin American dialects.
Loss or vocalization of final dental fricatives is a feature of historical development in certain varieties.
